title: hypericum relictum
intro: Hypericum relictum, also known as the sea lavender or narrow-leaved St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant species within the Hypericum genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Hypericum relictum include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally or applied topically to the skin to harness its medicinal properties.
